signals.title: GBP/NZD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Carry
signals.text: NZD is the weakest G8 currency this week, averaging -0.809% against its peers, while GBP is the strongest at +0.474%. The leader-to-laggard spread of 1.283% signals a strong bilateral theme. This divergence is driven by a significant carry advantage for GBP/NZD, with BoE 3.75% vs RBNZ 2.25% — +150bp carry premium favoring GBP. Retail sentiment shows an extreme 93% short GBP/NZD, indicating retail is fading the institutional carry trade. Watch for RBNZ's next meeting on 8 Jul for any shift in stance that is likely to impact the carry differential.

signals.title: USD/JPY — NFP + BoJ Intervention Risk + COT-OIS Divergence
signals.text: USD/JPY at 159.92 is testing the 160.00 intervention watch level ahead of US Nonfarm Payrolls (NFP) data today, which is expected to show a stable labor market. The 288bp Fed-BoJ OIS carry premium (SOFR 3.61% vs TONA 0.73%) continues to sustain the bid. JPY COT LF net short −86,249 (week ending May 26) represents a crowded spec short position, diverging from the BoJ's 'Hike' bias. This creates significant short-squeeze risk if BoJ signals a hike at its next meeting on 16 Jun or if NFP surprises to the downside, triggering a USD pullback. Options market shows moderate put skew (25d RR -1.15), indicating hedging against JPY strength despite the carry bid.

signals.title: EUR/USD — NFP + ECB Hawkishness + COT-OIS Divergence
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1644 is showing resilience ahead of US NFP, with ECB hawkishness offsetting Fed strength. The ECB has a 'Hike' bias, yet EUR COT LF net short −26,311 (week ending May 26) indicates speculative positioning is fading this hawkish stance. This COT-OIS divergence creates short-squeeze risk for EUR/USD if the ECB delivers on its hawkish bias at its next meeting on 11 Jun or if NFP disappoints, weakening the USD. Options market shows elevated vol premium (IV 7.1% vs HV30 3.9%, VRP +3.2%) and mild put skew (25d RR -0.32), suggesting hedging demand for EUR downside despite the potential for a squeeze.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + CESI Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7144 is facing headwinds from a moderate negative CESI bias for AUD (net -8 misses), indicating data is consistently disappointing expectations. This contrasts with AUD COT LF net long +58,041 (week ending May 26), which is toward the upper end of the historical range (long%=76.1%). The divergence between strong speculative long positioning and weakening economic data suggests potential for an unwind if data continues to underperform. The 74bp AUD carry premium (AONIA 4.35% vs SOFR 3.61%) provides some support, but a sustained negative CESI could challenge this. Watch for the next RBA meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 16 Jun for any shift in policy guidance.
